name: codex-continuity
description: Thin continuity adapter for Codex. Adds prior-session retrieval, transcript-backed pre-compact checkpoints, and stop-time note settling over the stock memories workspace.

interface.shortDescription: Thin continuity tooling for Codex memory and sessions.
interface.longDescription: Adds continuity workflow on top of stock Codex memory without modifying codex-rs: capture skills write structured ad-hoc notes to ~/.codex/memories/extensions/ad_hoc/notes/, a retrieval MCP sidecar exposes project summary, search, related-file lookup, recent-decision lookup, session inventory, session health checks, raw rollout archive backup, session context, note draft, note settling, explicit core memory update draft/apply, and workspace status tools. SessionStart injects a thin prior-session context projection through additionalContext and writes a lightweight health marker, UserPromptSubmit injects prompt-time prior context without writing process files and writes a lightweight health marker, PostToolUse archives raw rollout state after tool completion, PreCompact reads the Codex transcript path and writes a legal ad-hoc checkpoint note before Codex replaces active history, PostCompact archives raw rollout state after compaction, and Stop settles final outcomes from the final assistant message plus transcript tail into ad-hoc notes or hash-protected ad-hoc note updates. Codex transcript and rollout files remain the full-fidelity source for active-session process facts; ad-hoc notes are only derived checkpoints for stock Codex consolidation. The no-loss hardening layer inventories active sessions and archived_sessions, reports malformed or mismatched rollout records, and automatically archives raw rollout files from PostToolUse, PreCompact, PostCompact, and Stop under ~/.codex/codex-continuity/raw_archive/ without injecting them into memory. Stable session outcomes still surface reviewable core-memory promotion drafts for MEMORY.md or memory_summary.md. Codex host runtime remains the source of truth for additional_context role handling, deduplication, truncation, and compaction; codex-continuity stays focused on session retrieval, transcript-backed checkpointing, digesting, settling, no-loss backup, and promotion workflow. Core MEMORY.md and memory_summary.md are not forbidden: intentional corrections use explicit reviewed draft/apply with hash protection; automatic hooks remain review-first to avoid silently overwriting consolidation output.
defaultPrompt: Finish this task, capture the root cause, and use memory retrieval when prior context may help.
Fix this bug, save a structured ad-hoc memory note, and search related files and past decisions in memory.
